 {"id":"bd-0134cc5a","title":"Fix auto-import creating duplicates instead of updating issues","description":"ROOT CAUSE: server_export_import_auto.go line 221 uses ResolveCollisions: true for ALL auto-imports. This is wrong.

Problem:
- ResolveCollisions is for branch merges (different issues with same ID)
- Auto-import should UPDATE existing issues, not create duplicates
- Every git pull creates NEW duplicate issues with different IDs
- Two agents ping-pong creating endless duplicates

Evidence:
- 31 duplicate groups found (bd duplicates)
- bd-236-246 are duplicates of bd-224-235
- Both agents keep pulling and creating more duplicates
- JSONL file grows endlessly with duplicates

The Fix:
Change checkAndAutoImportIfStale in server_export_import_auto.go:
- Remove ResolveCollisions: true (line 221)
- Use normal import logic that updates existing issues by ID
- Only use ResolveCollisions for explicit bd import --resolve-collisions

Impact: Critical - makes beads unusable for multi-agent workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-27T21:48:57.733846-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.21084-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-27T22:26:40.627239-07:00"}

Approaches:
1. Mechanical: Exact title/description matching
2. Embeddings: Cosine similarity (cheap, scalable)
3. AI: LLM-based semantic comparison (expensive, accurate)

Uses embeddings by default for >100 issues.

 {"id":"bd-is6m","title":"Add gate checking to Deacon patrol loop","description":"Integrate gate checking into Deacon's patrol cycle.

## Patrol Integration
```go
func (d *Deacon) checkGates(ctx context.Context) {
    gates, _ := d.store.ListOpenGates(ctx)
    
    for _, gate := range gates {
        // Check timeout
        if time.Since(gate.CreatedAt) > gate.Timeout {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "timeout")
            d.closeGate(gate, "timed out")
            continue
        }
        
        // Check condition
        if d.checkCondition(gate.AwaitType, gate.AwaitID) {
            d.notifyWaiters(gate, "cleared")
            d.closeGate(gate, "condition met")
        }
    }
}
```

## Note
This task is in Gas Town (gt), not beads.  {"id":"bd-iw11","title":"Fix TestCheckMetadataVersionTracking test threshold","description":"The test 'TestCheckMetadataVersionTracking/slightly_outdated_version' fails because:

- Test uses version 0.35.0 as 'slightly outdated'
- Current version is 0.45.0 (10 minor versions ahead)

The test's version threshold needs updating, or the threshold logic needs adjusting.

## Error
```
doctor_test.go:984: Expected status ok, got warning (message: Last recorded version is very old: 0.35.0 (current: 0.45.0))
```

## Fix options
1. Update test version from 0.35.0 to 0.43.0 (within threshold)
2.  {"id":"bd-kzxd","title":"Sync protection mechanism resurrects deleted issues","description":"During bd sync, the 'Protecting N issue(s) from left snapshot' logic resurrects issues that are in the deletions manifest, causing UNIQUE constraint failures on subsequent syncs.

## Reproduction
1. Delete an issue with bd delete (creates tombstone + deletions.jsonl entry)
2. Run bd sync - succeeds
3. Run bd sync again - fails with 'UNIQUE constraint failed: issues.id'

## Root Cause
The protection mechanism (designed to prevent data loss during 3-way merge) keeps a snapshot of issues before sync. When importing after pull, it restores issues from this snapshot even if they're in the deletions manifest.

## Observed Behavior
- bd-3pd was deleted and added to deletions.jsonl
- First sync exports tombstone, commits, pulls, imports - succeeds
- Second sync: protection restores bd-3pd from left snapshot
- Import tries to create bd-3pd which already exists → UNIQUE constraint error

## Expected Behavior
Issues in deletions manifest should NOT be restored by protection mechanism.

## Workaround
Manually delete from DB: sqlite3 .beads/beads.db 'DELETE FROM issues WHERE id = "bd-xxx"'

## Files to Investigate
- cmd/bd/sync.go - protection logic
- cmd/bd/snapshot_manager.go - left snapshot handling
- internal/importer/importer.go - import with protection","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T10:11:02.550663-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T10:20:51.651662-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T10:20:51.651662-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-l5gq","title":"Optimize test suite performance - cut runtime by 50%+","description":"## Problem
Test suite takes ~20.8 seconds, with 95% of time spent in just 2 tests:
- TestHashIDs_MultiCloneConverge: 11.08s (53%)
- TestHashIDs_IdenticalContentDedup: 8.78s (42%)

Both tests in beads_hash_multiclone_test.go perform extensive Git operations (bare repos, multiple clones, sync rounds).

## Goal
Cut total test time by at least 50% (to ~10 seconds or less).

## Analysis
Tests already have some optimizations:
- --shared --depth=1 --no-tags for fast cloning
- Disabled hooks, gc, fsync
- Support -short flag

## Impact
- Faster development feedback loop
- Reduced CI costs and time
- Better developer experience","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-11-04T01:23:14.410648-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.683213-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-04T11:23:13.683213-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-l6yk","title":"bd mol burn should work on mols, not just wisps","description":"Currently `bd mol burn` only works on wisps (Ephemeral=true):

```
$ bd mol burn bd-mol-p7i
Error: molecule bd-mol-p7i is not a wisp (Ephemeral=false)
Hint: mol burn only works with wisp molecules
      Use 'bd delete' to remove non-wisp issues
```

The name 'burn' implies destruction - it should work on any molecule, not just wisps. When called on a regular mol, it should convert to `bd delete --cascade`.

**Proposed behavior:**
- If wisp: current behavior (delete without tombstones)
- If mol: `bd delete <id> --cascade` (delete with tombstones)

This makes the metaphor consistent: burn = destroy a molecule, regardless of phase.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-29T17:05:08.115592-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-29T17:10:19.034979-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-29T17:10:19.034979-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented: mol burn now works on both wisps and mols"}
